630	\begin{lemma}
631	
632	\leanok
633	[Inverse of an invertible module]
634	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
636	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
637	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
638	  % NOTE: off the critical path for the relative Picard group law. The group
639	  % law uses the tensor-invertibility predicate IsInvertible
640	  % (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible), which carries the inverse existentially,
641	  % so no separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is required. Retained only
642	  % as a supplement on the geometric line-bundle subcategory.
643	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
644	  bundle. The dual
645	  \[
646	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
647	  \]
648	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
649	  isomorphism
650	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
651	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
652	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
653	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
654	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
655	\end{lemma}
656	
657	\begin{proof}
658	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
659	  Locally trivial of rank one is preserved by
660	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
661	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
662	  The contraction map
663	  \(L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is a
664	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
665	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
666	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
667	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
668	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)
669	  is then standard (Stacks tag 01CR).
670	\end{proof}

672	\begin{lemma}
673	
674	\leanok
675	[Closure of \(\otimes\) on the \(\otimes\)-invertible carrier
676	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
677	  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
678	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
679	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
680	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
681	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
682	  % NOTE: the Lean target `tensorObjOnProduct` provides ONLY the
683	  % operation-closure on the subtype (that the tensor product of two
684	  % `LineBundle.OnProduct` objects is again one). The remaining group-law
685	  % data stated in this block --- unit-membership of O_{C×T}, the
686	  % dual/inverse being a two-sided tensor inverse, and the
687	  % associativity/unit/commutativity existence-of-iso facts --- are SEPARATE
688	  % items, NOT delivered by `tensorObjOnProduct`. Under the
689	  % tensor-invertibility re-point the carrier `OnProduct` will be
690	  % re-instantiated as `{ M : (pullback πC πT).Modules // IsInvertible M }`
691	  % (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible); the coherence isos
692	  % (lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso)
693	  % and the units-of-monoid engine (lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup) supply
694	  % the group-law data, so none of these items is blocked on
695	  % \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. `IsLocallyTrivial` is retained as a
696	  % separate predicate, connected to `IsInvertible` only by an
697	  % off-critical-path equivalence.
698	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes. Under the planned re-point of
699	  \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, the carrier is the
700	  \(\otimes\)-invertible subtype
701	  \[
702	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
703	    \;\;=\;\;
704	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
705	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M \,\bigr\}
706	  \]
707	  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}. The bifunctor
708	  \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
709	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to this subtype:
710	  if \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M\) and \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\,M'\) then
711	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes_{C \times_S T} M')\); the unit
712	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible; and every invertible module
713	  carries its own tensor inverse. Hence the isomorphism classes of the subtype
714	  form the units group of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} on

717	
718	\begin{proof}
719	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
720	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
721	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
722	  Closure under \(\otimes\) is the units-closed-under-multiplication statement of
723	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}: if \(M \otimes N \cong \mathcal{O}\)
724	  and \(M' \otimes N' \cong \mathcal{O}\), then
725	  \((M \otimes M') \otimes (N \otimes N') \cong \mathcal{O}\) by the coherence
726	  isomorphisms, so \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M \otimes M')\) with witness
727	  \(N \otimes N'\). The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) is invertible with
728	  witness itself (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the tensor inverse is
729	  carried by the predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Thus the
730	  subtype is a submonoid all of whose elements are units --- the abelian group
731	  of iso-classes that is the units group of
732	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} restricted to \(C \times_S T\).
733	\end{proof}

735	\begin{lemma}
736	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
737	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
738	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
739	  % NOTE: no Lean declaration yet. NOT blocked on lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso:
740	  % pullback preserves the structure sheaf and tensor products (and hence
741	  % tensor-invertibility) by the standard pullback-tensor compatibility of
742	  % O-modules (Stacks lemma-tensor-product-pullback / lemma-pullback-invertible),
743	  % independently of the open-immersion restriction-compatibility iso.
744	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
745	  functor
746	  \[
747	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
748	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
749	  \]
750	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
751	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
752	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
753	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
754	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
755	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
756	  canonical isomorphism
757	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
758	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
759	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
760	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
761	\end{lemma}
762	
763	\begin{proof}
764	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
765	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
766	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
767	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
768	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
769	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
770	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
771	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
772	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
773	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
774	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
775	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
776	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
777	  same cover.
778	\end{proof}

780	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
781	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
782	
783	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
784	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
785	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
786	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
787	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
788	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
789	design template. The inverse of an invertible object is carried by the predicate
790	itself, so neither the open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
791	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a separate tensor-inverse lemma is on the
792	critical path.
793	
794	\begin{definition}[\(\otimes\)-invertibility of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module]
795	  \label{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}
796	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible}
797	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
798	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
799	  % modules), Definition 01CS (definition-invertible) + Lemma 0B8K
800	  % (lemma-invertible, the "$\exists\,\mathcal{N}$" characterisation)
801	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4079).
802	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
803	  % "\begin{definition}
804	  % \label{definition-invertible}
805	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
806	  % {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
807	  % of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that
808	  % the functor
809	  % $$
810	  % \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \longrightarrow \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X),\quad
811	  % \mathcal{F} \longmapsto \mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}
812	  % $$
813	  % is an equivalence of categories. We say that $\mathcal{L}$ is
814	  % {\it trivial} if it is isomorphic as an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
815	  % to $\mathcal{O}_X$.
816	  % \end{definition}
817	  % [\dots]
818	  % \begin{lemma}
819	  % \label{lemma-invertible}
820	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Let $\mathcal{L}$
821	  % be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Equivalent are
822	  % \begin{enumerate}
823	  % \item $\mathcal{L}$ is invertible, and
824	  % \item there exists an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{N}$
825	  % such that
826	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X$.
827	  % \end{enumerate}
828	  % In this case $\mathcal{L}$ is locally a direct summand of a finite free
829	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-module and the module $\mathcal{N}$ in (2) is isomorphic to
830	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
831	  % \end{lemma}"
832	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
833	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CS and Lemma~0B8K. An invertible
834	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is one for which \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
835	  (-)\) is an equivalence; equivalently there exists \(\mathcal{N}\) with
836	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), and
837	  then \(\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
838	  \mathcal{O}_X)\).}
839	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). We say \(M\)
840	  is \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertible} when it admits a tensor inverse: there is an
841	  object \(N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) together with an isomorphism
842	  \[
843	    M \otimes_X N \;\cong\;
844	      \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
845	      \;=\; \mathcal{O}_X ,
846	  \]
847	  i.e.\ \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M) :\equiv \exists\, N,\
848	  \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(M \otimes_X N \cong
849	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)\), with
850	  \(\otimes_X\) the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}. The
851	  predicate is a \emph{proposition} carrying its inverse witness existentially;
852	  consequently the dual/inverse needed by the group law is definitional and no
853	  separate ``a tensor inverse exists'' lemma is consumed downstream. This is the
854	  scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\), transported
855	  one categorical level up to \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with the substrate
856	  \(\otimes_X\).
857	\end{definition}
858	
859	\begin{lemma}[The commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes and its units]
860	  \label{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}
861	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid}
862	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
863	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
864	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
865	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
866	  % modules), Definition 01CX (definition-pic) + Lemma lemma-constructions-
867	  % invertible (tensor of invertibles is invertible; dual is the inverse)
868	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200-L4213 and L4350-L4357).
869	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
870	  % "\begin{lemma}
871	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
872	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
873	  % \begin{enumerate}
874	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$, $\mathcal{N}$ are invertible
875	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, then so is
876	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{N}$.
877	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
878	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
879	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
880	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
881	  % is an isomorphism.
882	  % \end{enumerate}
883	  % \end{lemma}
884	  % [\dots]
885	  % \begin{definition}
886	  % \label{definition-pic}
887	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
888	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the
889	  % abelian group whose elements are isomorphism classes of
890	  % invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules, with addition
891	  % corresponding to tensor product.
892	  % \end{definition}"
893	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
894	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
895	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
896	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit
897	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and inverse the dual; the tensor product of invertibles is
898	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
899	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of objects of
900	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) under the substrate tensor product
901	  \(\otimes_X\) form a commutative monoid: multiplication is
902	  \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\), well-defined on isomorphism classes
903	  because \(\otimes_X\) is a bifunctor
904	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
905	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically);
906	  the unit is \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and associativity, unitality and
907	  commutativity are the coherence isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
908	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, read as
909	  equalities of iso-classes. An element \([M]\) is a \emph{unit} of this monoid
910	  iff \(M\) is \(\otimes\)-invertible
911	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}): a tensor inverse \(N\) with
912	  \(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) is exactly a multiplicative inverse of
913	  \([M]\). Hence the group of units of this commutative monoid is the abelian
914	  group of iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules ---
915	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR). This mirrors Mathlib's
916	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\)
917	  and the transported \(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), used here as the design
918	  template; the scheme-level construction is genuinely absent from Mathlib at
919	  the pinned commit.
920	\end{lemma}
921	
922	\begin{proof}
923	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
924	        lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
925	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
926	  Well-definedness of \([M]\cdot[N] := [M \otimes_X N]\) on iso-classes is the
927	  bifunctoriality of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}. The
928	  commutative-monoid axioms are propositions about iso-classes and are
929	  discharged by the coherence isomorphisms: associativity by
930	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unit laws by \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso},
931	  commutativity by \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}; no pentagon, triangle or
932	  hexagon identity is invoked, since each axiom asserts only the
933	  \emph{existence} of an isomorphism. By definition of a unit in a commutative
934	  monoid, \([M]\) is invertible iff there is \([N]\) with
935	  \([M \otimes_X N] = [\mathcal{O}_X]\), i.e.\ iff \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\)
936	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}). Therefore the units subgroup is
937	  precisely the iso-classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible modules, and it is an
938	  abelian group --- the Picard group of \(X\) (Stacks tag 01CR). The whole
939	  assembly follows the Mathlib \(\mathtt{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\cdots))\)
940	  template.
941	\end{proof}

943	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
944	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
945	
946	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
947	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
948	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
949	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
950	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
951	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
952	
953	\begin{theorem}
954	
955	\leanok
956	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
957	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
958	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
959	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
960	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
961	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
962	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
963	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
964	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
965	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
966	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
967	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
968	
969	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
970	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
971	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
972	  % groups)
973	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
974	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
975	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
976	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
977	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
978	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
979	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
980	  % \end{dfn}
981	  %  [\dots]
982	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
983	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
984	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
985	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
986	  % topologies  by
987	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
988	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
989	  %  \end{dfn}"
990	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
991	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
992	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
993	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
994	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
995	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
996	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
997	  \[
998	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
999	  \]

1000	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1001	  abelian-group structure with addition
1002	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1003	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1004	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1005	  inverse
1006	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1007	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1008	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1009	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1010	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1011	  map
1012	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1013	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1014	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1015	\end{theorem}
1016	
1017	\begin{proof}
1018	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1019	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1020	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1021	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1022	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1023	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1024	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1025	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1026	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1027	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1028	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1029	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1030	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1031	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1032	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1033	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1034	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1035	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1036	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1037	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1038	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1039	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1040	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1041	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1042	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1043	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1044	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1045	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1046	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1047	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1048	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1049	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1050	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1051	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1052	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1053	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1054	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1055	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1056	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1057	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1058	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1059	
1060	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1061	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1062	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1063	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1064	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1065	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1066	
1067	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1068	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1069	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1070	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1071	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1072	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1073	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1074	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1075	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1076	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1077	\end{proof}
1078	
1079	\section{LOC estimates and prover-lane sequencing}
1080	\label{sec:tensorobj_loc_estimates}
1081	
1082	The substrate splits into three logical pieces; each is one prover lane.
1083	The route is deliberately \emph{lighter} than a monoidal-category build:
1084	no pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence is discharged, and no
1085	\(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure is constructed (cf.\
1086	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
1087	
1088	\paragraph{Piece 1: definition + functoriality + well-definedness on
1089	iso-classes
1090	(\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}).}
1091	The Lean target file is the new
1092	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}. The body
1093	consists of:
1094	\begin{itemize}
1095	  \item a definition
1096	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} taking two
1097	    \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) objects and producing one, via
1098	    \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) on the underlying
1099	    presheaves and the sheafification functor on the small Zariski site;
1100	  \item the functorial action on morphisms (bifunctoriality, identity
1101	    and composition laws), enough to transport an isomorphism
1102	    \(L \cong L_0\) to \(L \otimes M \cong L_0 \otimes M\) --- i.e.\
1103	    well-definedness of the operation on isomorphism classes.
1104	\end{itemize}
1105	Estimate: approximately 30--60 LOC project-side. (No full natural-iso
1106	associator/unitor/braiding \emph{coherence} data is needed here; only the
1107	bifunctorial action and the iso-transport.)
1108	
1109	\paragraph{Piece 2: the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate + the three
1110	coherence isomorphisms
1111	(\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1112	       lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
1113	       lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
1114	       lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}).}
1115	The same file, building on Piece 1. The body consists of:
1116	\begin{itemize}
1117	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
1118	    \texttt{IsInvertible}, a \(\mathrm{Prop}\) carrying a tensor inverse
1119	    existentially (the \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) analogue), so the inverse
1120	    needed by the group law is definitional;
1121	  \item the three monoidal coherence isomorphisms --- associator, left/right
1122	    unitors, braiding --- each built by the cheap pattern
1123	    \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) applied to the presheaf-level
1124	    \(\alpha\)/\(\lambda\)/\(\rho\)/\(\beta\) on
1125	    \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) (the unitors composing additionally with
1126	    the sheafification counit at the structure-sheaf unit, exactly as the
1127	    existing \texttt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} / \texttt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}
1128	    helpers), with no recourse to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\).
1129	\end{itemize}
1130	Estimate: the unitors and the braiding are \(\approx 15\) LOC each (the bare
1131	\(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\) pattern); the \(\otimes\)-invertibility
1132	predicate is a few lines. The associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}) is the
1133	one exception: because it nests a sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on
1134	both sides, it additionally requires the \emph{sheafification--tensor absorption}
1135	isomorphism
1136	\(\mathtt{sheafification}(\mathtt{sheafification}(A).\mathtt{val} \otimes^{p} B)
1137	\cong \mathtt{sheafification}(A \otimes^{p} B)\) (whiskering-stability of the
1138	sheafification localizer for an arbitrary module), the same stability flagged in
1139	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}; it should be scheduled as a separate
1140	helper ahead of the consumer. Total for Piece 2: roughly \(50\)--\(70\) LOC if the
1141	absorption iso is cheap, more if it must be built from scratch. This is still
1142	materially lighter than a full coherence-axiom discharge: no
1143	pentagon/triangle/hexagon identity is proved, and the open-immersion
1144	restriction-compatibility isomorphism is \emph{not} needed.
1145	
1146	\paragraph{Piece 3: lift + consumer
1147	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1148	       lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1149	       thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}).}
1150	The lift lemmas live in the same
1151	\texttt{TensorObjSubstrate.lean} file; the consumer instance
1152	\(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup\_via\_tensorObj}\)
1153	lives back in
1154	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean}. The body
1155	consists of:
1156	\begin{itemize}
1157	  \item the bifunctor and the four iso lemmas restrict to the
1158	    \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (Piece 3a, $\approx 10$ LOC);
1159	  \item \(\pi_T^*\) carries tensor to tensor and unit to unit (Piece 3b,
1160	    $\approx 10$--$20$ LOC);
1161	  \item the consumer \texttt{addCommGroup\_via\_tensorObj} instance
1162	    establishing \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, assembling the
1163	    \(\AddCommGroup\) on iso-classes directly from the four iso lemmas
1164	    and descending it via \texttt{QuotientAddGroup} on the
1165	    \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) subgroup (Piece 3c, $\approx 10$--$20$ LOC).
1166	\end{itemize}
1167	Estimate: approximately 20--40 LOC project-side total.
1168	
1169	\paragraph{Total + sequencing.} Roughly 80--150 LOC project-side --- lighter
1170	than a monoidal-category route, since the coherence discharge and the
1171	\(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) build are avoided entirely. The recommended
1172	prover-lane sequence is Piece 1 → Piece 2 → Piece 3, because:
1173	\begin{itemize}
1174	  \item Piece 2 consumes the bifunctor and iso-transport of Piece 1;
1175	    closing it before Piece 1 is impossible.
1176	  \item Piece 3a--3b restrict the four existence-of-iso lemmas of Piece 2 to
1177	    the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype and to \(\pi_T^*\); they
1178	    depend on Piece 2 but \emph{not} on any monoidal-coherence data.
1179	  \item Piece 3c is the only piece in
1180	    \texttt{RelPicFunctor.lean}; all other pieces live in the new
1181	    file \texttt{TensorObjSubstrate.lean}. Once Pieces 1--3b are in
1182	    place, Piece 3c is a short bridging instance completing
1183	    \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
1184	\end{itemize}
1185	
1186	Once Piece 3c is established, the five dependent declarations
1187	\texttt{PicSharp},
1188	\texttt{PicSharp.functorial},
1189	\texttt{PicSharp.presheaf},
1190	\texttt{PicSharp.etSheaf},
1191	\texttt{PicSharp.etSheaf\_group\_structure}
1192	each depend on \cref{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj} and close
1193	directly against it, following the patterns of chapter
1194	\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
1195	Completing Piece 3c closes A.1.c.SubT and clears the dependency of
1196	A.2.c (chapter \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) on chapter
1197	\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.

1199	\section{Out of scope}
1200	\label{sec:tensorobj_out_of_scope}
1201	
1202	The following are downstream of this chapter and live in dedicated
1203	sibling chapters; this chapter does \emph{not} touch them:

1206	    (\cref{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification,

1217	  \item \textbf{Identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)} (A.3,

1225	    the substrate of this chapter is project-side, intentionally; a
1226	    parallel Mathlib upstream PR is welcome but not on the critical
1227	    path. Once the substrate ships in Mathlib, the
1228	    \texttt{TensorObjSubstrate.lean} file can be slimmed to a single

1	# Analogy: the invertible-scoped associator for `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` — which realization avoids the `MonoidalClosed` wall
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ts-assoc-gate210
8	
9	## Iteration
10	210
11	
12	## Question
13	Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
14	hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
15	scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
16	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
17	(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
18	on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
19	avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?
20	
21	## Project artifact(s)
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
23	  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
24	- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
25	  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
26	  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
27	  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
28	  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
29	- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
30	  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
31	  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
32	  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
33	- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
34	  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
35	- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.
36	
37	## Decisions identified
38	
39	### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator
40	
41	The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
42	`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
43	Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
44	**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
45	of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.
46	
47	- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
48	  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
49	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
50	  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
51	  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
52	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
53	  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
54	  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
55	- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
56	  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
57	  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
58	  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
59	  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
60	  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
61	  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
62	  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
63	  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
64	  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
65	- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
66	  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
67	  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
68	  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
69	  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
70	- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
71	  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
72	  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
73	  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
74	  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
75	  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
76	  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
77	  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
78	  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
79	  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
80	  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
81	- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
82	  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
83	  `MonoidalClosed`).
84	
85	### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat
86	
87	- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
88	  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
89	  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
90	  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
91	- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
92	  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
93	  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
94	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
95	  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
96	  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
97	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
98	  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.
99	
100	## Recommendation
101	
102	Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
103	the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
104	`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
105	absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
106	`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
107	+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
108	API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.
109	
110	Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
111	residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
112	is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
113	"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
114	hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
115	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.
116	
117	**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,
118	so the lane is alive. But the directive's *favored* realization (1) IS a renamed wall — the bridge
119	to build is the flat-whiskering `J.W` stability, not the structure-sheaf gluing. The remaining
120	prover risk is bounded and elementary (the flat-whiskering bridge ≈ 30–80 LOC over the
121	locally-bijective API), NOT a multi-file `MonoidalClosed`/strong-monoidal-pushforward build.

1	# Analogy: the invertible-scoped associator for `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` — which realization avoids the `MonoidalClosed` wall
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	ts-assoc-gate210
8	
9	## Iteration
10	210
11	
12	## Question
13	Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
14	hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
15	scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
16	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
17	(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
18	on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
19	avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?
20	
21	## Project artifact(s)
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
23	  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
24	- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
25	  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
26	  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
27	  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
28	  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
29	- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
30	  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
31	  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
32	  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
33	- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
34	  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
35	- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.
36	
37	## Decisions identified
38	
39	### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator
40	
41	The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
42	`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
43	Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
44	**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
45	of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.
46	
47	- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
48	  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
49	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
50	  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
51	  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
52	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
53	  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
54	  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
55	- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
56	  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
57	  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
58	  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
59	  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
60	  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
61	  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
62	  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
63	  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
64	  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
65	- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
66	  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
67	  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
68	  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
69	  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
70	- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
71	  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
72	  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
73	  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
74	  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
75	  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
76	  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
77	  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
78	  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
79	  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
80	  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
81	- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
82	  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
83	  `MonoidalClosed`).
84	
85	### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat
86	
87	- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
88	  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
89	  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
90	  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
91	- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
92	  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
93	  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
94	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
95	  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
96	  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
97	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
98	  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.
99	
100	## Recommendation
101	
102	Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
103	the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
104	`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
105	absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
106	`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
107	+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
108	API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.
109	
110	Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
111	residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
112	is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
113	"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
114	hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
115	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.
116	
117	**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,
118	so the lane is alive. But the directive's *favored* realization (1) IS a renamed wall — the bridge
119	to build is the flat-whiskering `J.W` stability, not the structure-sheaf gluing. The remaining
120	prover risk is bounded and elementary (the flat-whiskering bridge ≈ 30–80 LOC over the
121	locally-bijective API), NOT a multi-file `MonoidalClosed`/strong-monoidal-pushforward build.
122

69	  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).

  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
  `MonoidalClosed`).

### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat

- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.

## Recommendation

Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.

Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.

**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,
so the lane is alive. But the directive's *favored* realization (1) IS a renamed wall — the bridge
to build is the flat-whiskering `J.W` stability, not the structure-sheaf gluing. The remaining
prover risk is bounded and elementary (the flat-whiskering bridge ≈ 30–80 LOC over the
locally-bijective API), NOT a multi-file `MonoidalClosed`/strong-monoidal-pushforward build.

# Analogy: the invertible-scoped associator for `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` — which realization avoids the `MonoidalClosed` wall

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
ts-assoc-gate210

## Iteration
210

## Question
Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator

The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.

- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
  `MonoidalClosed`).

### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat

- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.

# Analogy: the invertible-scoped associator for `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` — which realization avoids the `MonoidalClosed` wall

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
ts-assoc-gate210

## Iteration
210

## Question
Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator

The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.

- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify

  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.

  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
  `MonoidalClosed`).

### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat

- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,

  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.

## Recommendation

Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`

+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.

Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.

so the lane is alive. But the directive's *favored* realization (1) IS a renamed wall — the bridge

Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator

The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.

- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
  `MonoidalClosed`).

### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat

- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.

## Recommendation

Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.

Do **NOT** take realization (1): it reduces to the sorry'd `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, whose
residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)
is absent from Mathlib AND flatness-independent, so the invertible scope does not unlock it — the
"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.

**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,
so the lane is alive. But the directive's *favored* realization (1) IS a renamed wall — the bridge
to build is the flat-whiskering `J.W` stability, not the structure-sheaf gluing. The remaining
prover risk is bounded and elementary (the flat-whiskering bridge ≈ 30–80 LOC over the
locally-bijective API), NOT a multi-file `MonoidalClosed`/strong-monoidal-pushforward build.

# Analogy: the invertible-scoped associator for `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` — which realization avoids the `MonoidalClosed` wall

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
ts-assoc-gate210

## Iteration
210

## Question
Scope the `⊗_X` associator `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` to INVERTIBLE (locally-free rank-1,
hence flat) objects only — all the relative-Picard group law consumes. Under that
scope: (1) is the associator existence-iso buildable from PRESENT Mathlib WITHOUT
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`? (2) which of three realizations —
(1) local-trivialization gluing, (2) flat-exactness whiskerLeft, (3) `J.W.IsMonoidal`
on the flat subcategory — should we formalize? (3) does realization (1) genuinely
avoid the absorption-iso wall, or does the global gluing secretly re-invoke it?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:199–202 — `tensorObj M N :=
  sheafification(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:330–399 — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the restriction-
  compatibility iso) is a typed `sorry`; its residual (lines 357–398) is the
  H1/H2 decomposition: (H1) a presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` adjunction,
  (H2) `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is strong monoidal — both absent from
  Mathlib, both flatness-INDEPENDENT.
- `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:412–425 — `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` (blueprint
  `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`, the cited "already proven" precedent
  for realization (1)); its proof at L423 CALLS `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
  **`lean_verify` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set** — it is NOT actually proven.
- blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`:531–578 — `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`,
  currently ARBITRARY `M,N,P`, `% NOTE` admits the absorption-iso/`MonoidalClosed` route.
- blueprint L795 `def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`; L859 `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`.

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator

The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
